intro: Pseuderanthemum riedelianum, also known as Brazilian flag or Riedel's false hyptis, is a flowering plant species native to Brazil, characterized by its red and yellow flowers and distinctive growth habit. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various conditions, such as wounds, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Pseuderanthemum riedelianum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been identified as responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues, as well as to make topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
